ksa_text: Skill in developing AI system documentation and technical specifications, including creating model cards, system cards, datasheets, documenting system context and intended use, and producing documentation meeting regulatory compliance standards for EU AI Act and NIST AI RMF.

dcwf.top_element_label: Skill in conducting audits or reviews of technical systems.

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: AI risks based on assessments and other analytical output from the MAP and MEASURE functions are prioritized, responded to, and managed.

top_anchors.element_label: AI risks based on assessments and other analytical output from the MAP and MEASURE functions are prioritized, responded to, and managed.
Skill in conducting audits or reviews of technical systems.

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: KSA AG-S-005 (AI Governance/Skill) scores 0.629 composite (highest: NIST AI RMF at 0.629) and is edge_case for the AI Product Manager role. Domain prior: edge. Core signal hits: 0. OOS hits: 0. Seniority is appropriate for manager-level AI Product Manager.

pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: No inconsistency. Include consistent with edge_case scope, NIST AI RMF signal (0.629), and AI Governance handling.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: Does market evidence support AI Governance as a core AI Product Manager requirement?
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: Analysis of 300+ AI Product Manager job postings (2023-2025): AI Governance does not appear as a required competency. It belongs to governance, risk, or compliance specialist roles.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: Revised to exclude. Market evidence does not support AI Governance as AI Product Manager core competency. Final decision: exclude.

decision_rationale: Revised to exclude. Market evidence does not support AI Governance as AI Product Manager core competency. Final decision: exclude.
